title: CLAUDE.md
text: Three layered S3 class hierarchies, engine-specific subclasses (jmb_*, smb_*, tmb_*, pmb_*, lmb_*): mb_code — model code string identified template type. mb_code() detects type dispatches new_mb_code(). pars() extracts parameter names; rm_comments() strips comments parsing. mb_model — complete model specification: code (mb_code), select_data (column specs + transforms), center/scale (normalization), random_effects (named list factor vectors), fixed (regex monitored params), derived (character vector), modify_data/modify_new_data (custom functions), new_expr (predictive expression), drops (list scalar params can fixed 0), gen_inits, nthin. Created via model(code_string, ...). mb_analysis — fitted model result storing model, data, mcmcr (posterior samples), duration, logLik. Engine packages implement analyse1() return mb_analysis subclass. Collections: mb_analyses (data, multiple models), mb_meta_analysis (one model, multiple datasets), mb_meta_analyses.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: generics (coef, pars, IC, glance, tidy, predict, fitted, residuals, converged) follow pattern: single analysis → collection → meta-collection.
code: analyse(x, data, ...)   analyse.character()   → model(x) → analyse(model, data)   analyse.mb_model()    → analyse_data() → analyse1()   # engine implements this   analyse.mb_models()   → analyse_model() per model

title: CLAUDE.md
text: drop_pars() walks model code AST replace named scalar parameters 0, enabling automated model selection. make_all_models() generates combinations drops; backwards() runs stepwise elimination IC.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: select_rescale_data() — selects columns per select_data spec, applies centering/scaling modify_data() — converts data.frame named list via user-supplied function + numericize_factors() analyse1() (engine) — fits model, returns mb_analysis coef(), predict(), mcmc_derive() — extract results mcmcr posterior

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Global options control defaults (prefix mb.): mb.nchains, mb.niters, mb.nthin, mb.parallel, mb.quiet, mb.rhat (1.1), mb.esr (0.33), mb.conf_level (0.95), mb.fixed ("^[^e]"), mb.stan_engine. converged() checks rhat < mb.rhat ESR > mb.esr. IC() returns AICc frequentist analyses WAIC Bayesian. Model averaging coef.mb_analyses() uses IC weights.

text: Integration tests (test-zzz-analyse*.R) require jmbr/JAGS Stan installed slow. Pre-computed .RDS analysis objects inst/test-objects/ loaded many tests avoid re-fitting. Snapshots tests/testthat/_snaps/. unit tests follow pattern: construct minimal mb_code mb_model, call function, check expect_identical / expect_equal / expect_snapshot.

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: example fish count model, data counts 5 sites across 5 annual periods two treatments (control, restored), stream temperature continuous covariate. Sampling unbalanced: 4 sites monitored 5 years; 5th site sampled 2 years.
code: sites <- letters[1:5] years <- 1:5 treatments <- c("control", "restored")  design <- expand.grid(   site = sites,   annual = years,   treatment = treatments,   rep = 1:3,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   filter(!(site == "e" & annual %in% c(1, 2, 3))) |>   mutate(     site = factor(site, levels = sites),     annual = factor(annual, levels = years),     treatment = factor(treatment, levels = treatments),     temperature = rnorm(n(), mean = 12, sd = 2)   )  bIntercept_true <- log(40) bTreatment_true <- 0.5 bTemp_true <- 0.15 sSite_true <- 0.4 sSiteAnnual_true <- 0.2 bPhi_true <- 5  re_site <- setNames(rnorm(length(sites), 0, sSite_true), sites) re_site_annual <- matrix(   rnorm(length(sites) * length(years), 0, sSiteAnnual_true),   nrow = length(sites),   dimnames = list(sites, years) )  data <- design |>   mutate(     temp_c = (temperature - mean(temperature)) / sd(temperature),     log_mu = bIntercept_true +       bTreatment_true * (treatment == "restored") +       bTemp_true * temp_c +       re_site[as.character(site)] +       mapply(         function(s, a) re_site_annual[s, a],         as.character(site),         as.character(annual)       ),     count = as.integer(ran_neg_binom(       n(),       lambda = exp(log_mu),       theta = bPhi_true     ))   ) |>   select(count, site, annual, treatment, temperature)  head(data) #>   count site annual treatment temperature #> 1    28    a      1   control    14.74192 #> 2   237    b      1   control    10.87060 #> 3    11    c      1   control    12.72626 #> 4     3    d      1   control    13.26573 #> 5     0    a      2   control    12.80854 #> 6   452    b      2   control    11.78775

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: model defined two parts: Stan code (likelihood priors) R new_expr (used post-fitting predictions, residuals, sensitivity, log likelihood). select_data random_effects described notes follow. notes model() arguments: code: Stan model code. new_expr: R expression evaluated post-fitting MCMC draws. defines prediction[] (used predict()), fit[] (used fitted() residuals), log_lik[] (used model comparison prior-sensitivity tools). scale fit[] model-specific; define match res_* log_lik_* helpers expect. Scalar derived quantities eBaseCount eRestoredEffect can extracted directly predict(analysis, new_data = character(0), term = "eBaseCount"). select_data: named list declaring columns required data, validation checks type range values, optional rescaling transformations. Type specifications 1L integer, 1 numeric, factor("") factor, TRUE logical. explicit value-range check wanted, use range form c(0L, 100L); append NA allow missing values: c(0L, 100L, NA). trailing suffix name requests rescaling transformation; original column replaced transformed version analysis dataset. Available suffixes: * default choice continuous covariates. random_effects: tells embr factors random-effect parameter indexed . predict() uses decide zero parameters (see prediction article).
code: count_code <- " data {   int<lower=2> nObs;   int<lower=1> nsite;   int<lower=1> nannual;   int<lower=1> ntreatment;   array[nObs] int<lower=1> site;   array[nObs] int<lower=1> annual;   array[nObs] int<lower=1> treatment;   vector[nObs] temperature;   array[nObs] int<lower=0> count; } parameters {   real bIntercept;   vector[ntreatment - 1] bTreatment_dev;   real bTemp;   real<lower=0> sSite;   real<lower=0> sSiteAnnual;   real<lower=0> bPhi;   vector[nsite] z_bSite;   matrix[nsite, nannual] z_bSiteAnnual; } model {   vector[nsite] bSite = z_bSite * sSite;   matrix[nsite, nannual] bSiteAnnual = z_bSiteAnnual * sSiteAnnual;   vector[ntreatment] bTreatment;   bTreatment[1] = 0;   for (k in 2:ntreatment) bTreatment[k] = bTreatment_dev[k - 1];    vector[nObs] log_eCount;   for (i in 1:nObs) {     log_eCount[i] = bIntercept + bTreatment[treatment[i]] +                     bTemp * temperature[i] + bSite[site[i]] +                     bSiteAnnual[site[i], annual[i]];   }    bIntercept ~ normal(0, 5);   bTreatment_dev ~ normal(0, 5);   bTemp ~ normal(0, 5);   sSite ~ exponential(1);   sSiteAnnual ~ exponential(1);   bPhi ~ gamma(2, 0.5);   to_vector(z_bSite) ~ std_normal();   to_vector(z_bSiteAnnual) ~ std_normal();    count ~ neg_binomial_2_log(log_eCount, bPhi); } "  count_model <- model(   code = count_code,   new_expr = {     bSite <- z_bSite * sSite     bSiteAnnual <- z_bSiteAnnual * sSiteAnnual     bTreatment <- c(0, bTreatment_dev)     eBaseCount <- exp(bIntercept)     eRestoredEffect <- exp(bTreatment_dev[1])     for (i in 1:nObs) {       log(eCount[i]) <- bIntercept +         bTreatment[treatment[i]] +         bTemp * temperature[i] +         bSite[site[i]] +         bSiteAnnual[site[i], annual[i]]       prediction[i] <- eCount[i]       fit[i] <- eCount[i]       log_lik[i] <- log_lik_neg_binom(count[i], eCount[i], bPhi)     }     lprior[1] <- log_lik_norm(bIntercept, 0, 5)     lprior[2] <- sum(log_lik_norm(bTreatment_dev, 0, 5))     lprior[3] <- log_lik_norm(bTemp, 0, 5)     lprior[4] <- log_lik_exp(sSite, 1)     lprior[5] <- log_lik_exp(sSiteAnnual, 1)     lprior[6] <- log_lik_gamma(bPhi, 2, 0.5)   },   new_expr_vec = TRUE,   select_data = list(     count = 1L,     site = factor("a"),     annual = factor("a"),     treatment = factor("a"),     `temperature*` = c(0, 100)   ),   random_effects = list(     z_bSite = "site",     z_bSiteAnnual = c("site", "annual")   ) )

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: rstan default fallback stan_engine set; cmdstanr via stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc" recommended faster compilation better diagnostics. niters, niters_warmup, nchains typically set via set_analysis_mode(); specified explicitly per call keep example self-contained. coef() extracts fixed-effect scale parameters:
code: analysis <- analyse(   count_model,   data = data,   stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc",   nchains = 3L,   niters = 500L,   seed = 42L,   parallel = TRUE,   quiet = TRUE,   beep = FALSE ) #> Warning: 2 of 1500 (0.0%) transitions ended with a divergence. #> See https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings for details. #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>       n     K nchains niters nthin   ess  rhat converged num_divergent #>   <int> <int>   <int>  <int> <int> <int> <dbl> <lgl>             <dbl> #> 1   132     6       3    500     1   413  1.00 FALSE                 2 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: max_treedepth <int>, ebfmi <dbl>  glance(analysis) #> Warning: 2 of 1500 (0.0%) transitions ended with a divergence. #> See https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings for details. #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>       n     K nchains niters nthin   ess  rhat converged num_divergent #>   <int> <int>   <int>  <int> <int> <int> <dbl> <lgl>             <dbl> #> 1   132     6       3    500     1   413  1.00 FALSE                 2 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: max_treedepth <int>, ebfmi <dbl> coef(analysis, include_constant = FALSE, simplify = TRUE) |>   mutate(across(estimate:svalue, ~ signif(.x, 3))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   term           estimate   lower upper svalue #>   <term>            <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 bIntercept       3.56    2.46   4.56  10.6   #> 2 bPhi             0.18    0.138  0.232 10.6   #> 3 bTemp            0.0858 -0.37   0.502  0.471 #> 4 bTreatment_dev   0.501  -0.39   1.44   1.86  #> 5 sSite            0.606   0.0348 1.84  10.6   #> 6 sSiteAnnual      0.319   0.0156 1.26  10.6

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: following arguments cover typical use analyse.mb_model: Stan-options (niters_warmup, seed) ignored JAGS models. niters number saved samples per chain thinning, total iterations run. Per chain: Warmup iterations (discarded): niters_warmup Post-warmup iterations: niters * nthin Saved samples: niters (every nthin-th post-warmup iteration) Total iterations run: niters_warmup + niters * nthin Across chains saved posterior nchains * niters draws. nchains = 3, niters = 500, niters_warmup = 500, nthin = 2, chain runs 500 + 500 * 2 = 1500 iterations contributes 500 saved samples, 1500 total posterior draws.

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: set_analysis_mode() sets bundle session-wide defaults (via options) individual analyse() calls don’t need respecify arguments. mode controls nchains, niters, nthin, parallel, quiet, glance, beep, reanalysis loop (nreanalyses, rhat, esr, duration), conf_level. Typically set top analysis script. rhat maximum acceptable R-hat (closer 1 stricter); esr minimum effective sample rate (effective sample size / total samples; closer 1 stricter). Typical workflow: get_analysis_mode() returns current option values. explicit argument passed analyse() always overrides corresponding option.
code: set_analysis_mode("quick") # check that model runs without error analyse(count_model, data = data, stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc")  set_analysis_mode("report") # report-grade fits analyse(count_model, data = data, stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc")

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: stan_engine argument selects Stan backend. JAGS models always go rjags regardless argument. See ?analyse per-engine list embr-controlled vs pass-arguments. pathfinder fit model much faster less reliable, especially complex hierarchical models:
code: analysis_pf <- analyse(   count_model,   data = data,   stan_engine = "cmdstan-pathfinder",   niters = 500L,   seed = 42L,   quiet = TRUE,   beep = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>       n     K converged return_code #>   <int> <int> <lgl>           <int> #> 1   132     6 TRUE                0  coef(analysis_pf, include_constant = FALSE, simplify = TRUE) |>   filter(term %in% c("bIntercept", "bTreatment_dev[1]", "bTemp")) |>   mutate(across(estimate:svalue, ~ signif(.x, 3))) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   term       estimate  lower upper svalue #>   <term>        <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 bIntercept    3.62   3.13  3.85    8.97 #> 2 bTemp        -0.147 -0.277 0.361   1.97

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: Additional cmdstanr arguments passed .... example, tighten NUTS step size: init one embr-supplied argument can overridden via .... Supply zero-argument function returning named list initial values per chain:
code: analyse(   count_model,   data = data,   stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc",   adapt_delta = 0.95,   max_treedepth = 12,   seed = 42L ) init_fun <- function() list(bIntercept = log(40))  analyse(   count_model,   data = data,   stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc",   init = init_fun,   seed = 42L )

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: fit several model structures data, wrap models() call analyse() : result mb_analyses list. predict.mb_analyses() returns IC-weighted model-averaged predictions across list.
code: count_model_no_temp <- model(   code = count_code, # in practice, with bTemp dropped   new_expr = {     # ... no temperature term ...   },   select_data = list(     count = 1L,     site = factor("a"),     annual = factor("a"),     treatment = factor("a")   ),   random_effects = list(     z_bSite = "site",     z_bSiteAnnual = c("site", "annual")   ) )  candidate_models <- models(   with_temp = count_model,   without_temp = count_model_no_temp )  analyses <- analyse(   candidate_models,   data = data,   stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc",   seed = 42L )

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: quick prototyping without separate model() object, pass Stan JAGS code string directly analyse() select_data list: convenience wrapper: calls model() internally dispatches mb_model method. Use one-shot fits; use explicit model() form want reuse, modify, inspect model object.
code: analyse(   count_code,   data = data,   select_data = list(     count = 1L,     site = factor("a"),     annual = factor("a"),     treatment = factor("a"),     `temperature*` = 1   ),   stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc",   seed = 42L )

title: Fitting Models with analyse()
text: See ?analyse full per-engine argument documentation. See prediction article posterior predictive summaries, effect-size calculations, mcmc_derive / mcmc_derive_data tools derived quantities. coef(), glance(), residuals(), plot_residuals() standard inspection tools mb_analysis.

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: simple example fish count model fit Stan (via cmdstanr smbr2). simulated data 5 sites × 5 years × 2 treatments, unbalanced design (.e., sites years data).
code: count_code <- " data {   int<lower=2> nObs;   int<lower=1> nsite;   int<lower=1> nannual;   int<lower=1> ntreatment;   array[nObs] int<lower=1> site;   array[nObs] int<lower=1> annual;   array[nObs] int<lower=1> treatment;   vector[nObs] temperature;   array[nObs] int<lower=0> count; } parameters {   real bIntercept;   vector[ntreatment - 1] bTreatment_dev;   real bTemp;   real<lower=0> sSite;   real<lower=0> sAnnual;   real<lower=0> bPhi;   vector[nsite] z_bSite;   vector[nannual] z_bAnnual; } model {   vector[nsite] bSite = z_bSite * sSite;   vector[nannual] bAnnual = z_bAnnual * sAnnual;   vector[ntreatment] bTreatment;   bTreatment[1] = 0;   for (k in 2:ntreatment) bTreatment[k] = bTreatment_dev[k - 1];    vector[nObs] log_eCount;   for (i in 1:nObs) {     log_eCount[i] = bIntercept + bTreatment[treatment[i]] +                     bTemp * temperature[i] + bSite[site[i]] +                     bAnnual[annual[i]];   }    bIntercept ~ normal(0, 5);   bTreatment_dev ~ normal(0, 5);   bTemp ~ normal(0, 5);   sSite ~ exponential(1);   sAnnual ~ exponential(1);   bPhi ~ gamma(2, 0.5);   to_vector(z_bSite) ~ std_normal();   to_vector(z_bAnnual) ~ std_normal();    count ~ neg_binomial_2_log(log_eCount, bPhi); } "  count_model <- model(   code = count_code,   new_expr = {     bSite <- z_bSite * sSite     bAnnual <- z_bAnnual * sAnnual     bTreatment <- c(0, bTreatment_dev)     eBaseCount <- exp(bIntercept)     eRestoredEffect <- exp(bTreatment_dev[1])     for (i in 1:nObs) {       log(eCount[i]) <- bIntercept +         bTreatment[treatment[i]] +         bTemp * temperature[i] +         bSite[site[i]] +         bAnnual[annual[i]]       prediction[i] <- eCount[i]       fit[i] <- eCount[i]       unobserved[i] <- exp(         bIntercept +           bTreatment[treatment[i]] +           bTemp * temperature[i] +           rnorm(1, 0, sSite) +           rnorm(1, 0, sAnnual)       )       log_lik[i] <- log_lik_neg_binom(count[i], eCount[i], bPhi)     }   },   new_expr_vec = TRUE,   select_data = list(     count = 1L,     site = factor("a"),     annual = factor("a"),     treatment = factor("a"),     `temperature*` = 1   ),   random_effects = list(     z_bSite = "site",     z_bAnnual = "annual"   ) ) analysis <- analyse(   count_model,   data = data,   stan_engine = "cmdstan-mcmc",   nchains = 3L,   niters = 500L,   niters_warmup = 500L,   seed = 42L,   parallel = TRUE,   quiet = TRUE,   beep = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>       n     K nchains niters nthin   ess  rhat converged num_divergent #>   <int> <int>   <int>  <int> <int> <int> <dbl> <lgl>             <dbl> #> 1   132     6       3    500     1   414  1.00 FALSE                 0 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: max_treedepth <int>, ebfmi <dbl>  coef(analysis, include_constant = FALSE, simplify = TRUE) |>   mutate(across(estimate:svalue, ~ signif(.x, 3))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   term           estimate  lower upper svalue #>   <term>            <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 bIntercept        3.69  2.99   4.32   10.6  #> 2 bPhi              4.13  3.13   5.41   10.6  #> 3 bTemp             0.109 0.0203 0.193 what: Named list: zero a subset of random effects
title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: Passing named list overrides random_effects defined model(), restricting set parameters eligible zeroing listed. Parameters list kept literal value first level associated factor new_data. useful want marginalize one random effect keeping another specific level. call (site annual level 1 new_data): z_bSite listed site level 1, z_bSite zeroed (population-mean site, .e. averaged across sites). z_bAnnual listed, kept literal value annual = 1 — specific year-1 effect. gives prediction averaged across sites anchored year 1 specifically.
code: xnew_data(data, temperature) |>   predict(     analysis,     new_data = _,     random_effects = list(z_bSite = "site")   ) |>   head(3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 9 #>   count site  annual treatment temperature estimate lower upper svalue #>   <int> <fct> <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1    62 a     1      control          6.01     38.9  20.1  71.7   10.6 #> 2    62 a     1      control          6.41     39.7  20.6  72.8   10.6 #> 3    62 a     1      control          6.80     40.6  21.2  73.3   10.6

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: control row sits exactly 0%. restored row gives proportional change expected count.
code: ref_control <- xnew_data(data, xcast(treatment = "control"))  pred_prop <- xnew_data(data, treatment) |>   predict(analysis, new_data = _, ref_data = ref_control)  pred_prop #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   count site  annual treatment temperature estimate lower upper svalue #>   <int> <fct> <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1    62 a     1      control          11.9    0     0      0       0   #> 2    62 a     1      restored         11.9    0.734 0.472  1.07   10.6  plot_pointrange(pred_prop, treatment) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +   labs(y = "Change vs control", x = "Treatment")

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: Pass function whose first argument takes vector two numbers returns scalar summarize different transformation two posteriors. example, absolute difference reports effect count scale (restored minus control) rather percentage:
code: pred_diff <- xnew_data(data, treatment) |>   predict(     analysis,     new_data = _,     ref_data = ref_control,     ref_fun2 = function(x) x[2] - x[1]   )  pred_diff #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   count site  annual treatment temperature estimate lower upper svalue #>   <int> <fct> <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1    62 a     1      control          11.9      0     0     0      0   #> 2    62 a     1      restored         11.9     28.9  13.8  58.0   10.6  plot_pointrange(pred_diff, treatment) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +   labs(y = "Change vs control (count)", x = "Treatment")

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: Zeroing random effect gives prediction typical average group level, e.g., expected count average site, amongst-site variability removed. However, new, unobserved level uncertainty wider account amongst-level variation. done drawing new random effect population distribution, typically incorporate mean 0 (random effects centred 0) SD random effect, estimated. model’s new_expr already defines unobserved term : Setting term = "unobserved" tells predict() summarize named quantity new_expr instead default "prediction". Compare two scopes side--side:  Compatibility limits wider unobserved site, interval now reflects amongst-site variation addition parameter uncertainty.
code: unobserved[i] <- exp(bIntercept +   bTreatment[treatment[i]] +   bTemp * temperature[i] +   rnorm(1, 0, sSite) +   rnorm(1, 0, sAnnual)) typical <- xnew_data(data, treatment) |>   predict(analysis, new_data = _) |>   mutate(scope = "typical site")  unobserved <- xnew_data(data, treatment) |>   predict(analysis, new_data = _, term = "unobserved") |>   mutate(scope = "unobserved site")  bind_rows(typical, unobserved) #> # A tibble: 4 × 10 #>   count site  annual treatment temperature estimate lower upper svalue scope     #>   <int> <fct> <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <chr>     #> 1    62 a     1      control          11.9     40.1 19.9   75.3   10.6 typical … #> 2    62 a     1      restored         11.9     68.7 34.5  131.    10.6 typical … #> 3    62 a     1      control          11.9     41.4  9.38 170.    10.6 unobserv… #> 4    62 a     1      restored         11.9     72.2 15.8  302.    10.6 unobserv… bind_rows(typical, unobserved) |>   ggplot(aes(treatment, estimate, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, colour = scope)) +   geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(y = "Expected count", x = "Treatment", colour = NULL)

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: new_expr can replaced inline string. new_values supplies named scalar constants expression’s environment. example, convert counts biomass multiplying literature independently measured mean fish mass (g), without refitting count model: second call multiplies predicted count external mean mass 12 g, giving posterior expected biomass per site. Compatibility limits scale proportionally constant uncertainty attached.
code: xnew_data(data, site) |>   predict(analysis, new_data = _) |>   head(2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   count site  annual treatment temperature estimate lower upper svalue #>   <int> <fct> <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1    62 a     1      control          11.9     33.6  22.4  49.3   10.6 #> 2    62 b     1      control          11.9     63.2  42.0  90.9   10.6  predict(   analysis,   new_data = xnew_data(data, site),   new_expr = "     bSite <- z_bSite * sSite     for (i in 1:length(site)) {       prediction[i] <- exp(bIntercept + bSite[site[i]]) * mean_mass_g     }   ",   new_values = list(mean_mass_g = 12) ) |>   head(2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   count site  annual treatment temperature estimate lower upper svalue #>   <int> <fct> <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1    62 a     1      control          11.9     403.  269.  592.   10.6 #> 2    62 b     1      control          11.9     758.  503. 1091.   10.6

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: mcmc_derive_data() pairs MCMC samples per-row data returns mcmc_data object. group_by() + summarize() aggregates across rows within group per MCMC iteration produce per-group posterior distribution summaries.

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: default summary function sum, returns posterior total abundance per treatment across rows.
code: mcmc_derive_data(analysis, new_data = data, term = "^eCount$") |>   group_by(treatment) |>   summarize() |>   coef() #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   treatment estimate lower upper svalue #>   <fct>        <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 control      3101. 2712. 3569.   10.6 #> 2 restored     5250. 4613. 6059.   10.6

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: Provide function via .fun. example, coefficient variation expected count across rows within treatment:
code: mcmc_derive_data(analysis, new_data = data, term = "^eCount$") |>   group_by(treatment) |>   summarize(.fun = function(x) sd(x) / mean(x)) |>   coef() #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   treatment estimate lower upper svalue #>   <fct>        <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 control      0.461 0.374 0.552   10.6 #> 2 restored     0.492 0.393 0.596   10.6

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: mcmc_data objects come mcmc_derive_data(); hold raw per-row MCMC samples together associated data, without point-estimate--limits summarisation predict() performs. mcmcr::combine_samples() (mcmc_data method mcmcdata) joins two mcmc_data objects shared data columns applies function align MCMC draws row--row. natural use composing quantities derived independent analyses: example, multiplying abundance posterior mass-per-fish posterior get biomass, component fitted different dataset. Assume separate mass_analysis fitted size-measurement data (different observation model, site factor), analyses expose per-site quantity: names data columns join (site); rows present inputs paired. fun applied matched MCMC draws, prod gives row-wise product across iterations. result mcmc_data one row per joined key propagated posterior biomass. two analyses combine: MCMC dimensions must match. fits need number chains iterations (e.g. 3 chains × 500 saved draws). combine_samples() pairs draws position--position; mismatched dimensions error . columns must exist $data slots. Rows paired inner join, non-matching rows dropped silently. Make sure factor levels column types line . Within single analysis, prefer expressing composition directly new_expr; combine_samples() useful components come independently fitted models whose draws need joining.
code: # Abundance posterior per site, from the counts model fitted above abundance <- mcmc_derive_data(   analysis,   new_data = xnew_data(data, site),   term = "^eCount$" )  # Mass-per-fish posterior per site, from a separate analysis (sketch) mass_per_fish <- mcmc_derive_data(   mass_analysis,   new_data = xnew_data(mass_data, site),   term = "^eMass$" )  # Per-site biomass posterior: abundance * mass-per-fish biomass <- mcmcr::combine_samples(   abundance,   mass_per_fish,   by = "site",   fun = prod )

text: mcmc_derive() returns raw MCMC samples one scalar terms new_expr. mcmc_derive_data(), useful want work raw MCMC samples directly rather predict()’s estimate--limits summary.

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: Unlike predict(), term argument mcmc_derive() can accept regular expression. example, eBaseCount eRestoredEffect extracted one call: coef() run mcmc_derive() output get point estimates compatibility limits.
code: scalars <- mcmc_derive(   analysis,   new_data = character(0),   term = "^(eBaseCount|eRestoredEffect)$" )  coef(scalars) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   term            estimate lower upper svalue #>   <term>             <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 eBaseCount         40.1  19.9  75.3    10.6 #> 2 eRestoredEffect     1.73  1.47  2.07   10.6

title: Prediction with predict() and Related Functions
text: Quantities anticipate usually cleanest define directly new_expr pull predict(). Raw mcmcr arithmetic earns keep question requires composing outputs different post-fit operations — example, combining group-aggregated quantity mcmc_derive_data() scalar mcmc_derive(), producing summaries predict() (probability statements, custom posterior quantiles). First, get totals per treatment, extract row raw MCMC draws: posterior absolute difference total expected catch (restored minus control), summed observed design — count-scale effect size eRestoredEffect (multiplicative ratio) doesn’t quantify: posterior probability restoration adds least 2000 fish total — question predict() answer:
code: totals <- mcmc_derive_data(analysis, new_data = data, term = "^eCount$") |>   group_by(treatment) |>   summarize()  restored_total <- as.mcmcr(filter(totals, treatment == "restored"))[[1]] control_total <- as.mcmcr(filter(totals, treatment == "control"))[[1]] extra_catch <- restored_total - control_total coef(extra_catch) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   term      estimate lower upper svalue #>   <term>       <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 parameter    2147. 1450. 3010.   10.6 mean(extra_catch >= 2000) #> [1] 0.6606667

Non-MCMC engines use gen_inits(). nthin niters_warmup ignored non-MCMC engines. pathfinder, variational, laplace, niters sets number draws approximated posterior.

title: Analyse Models — analyse
text: Calls cmdstanr::sample(). embr-controlled: chains, parallel_chains, iter_warmup, iter_sampling, thin, data, seed, init, show_messages, show_exceptions. Common pass-throughs: adapt_delta, max_treedepth, step_size, refresh, output_dir, save_warmup.

title: Analyse Models — analyse
text: Calls cmdstanr::pathfinder(). embr-controlled: data, seed, init, draws (niters), show_messages, show_exceptions. Common pass-throughs: num_paths, history_size, max_lbfgs_iters, psis_resample, tol_rel_grad.

title: Analyse Models — analyse
text: Calls cmdstanr::variational(). embr-controlled: data, seed, init, draws (niters), show_messages, show_exceptions. Common pass-throughs: algorithm ("meanfield" "fullrank"), iter, tol_rel_obj, eval_elbo, eta.

title: Analyse Models — analyse
text: Calls cmdstanr::optimize(). embr-controlled: data, seed, init, show_messages, show_exceptions. Common pass-throughs: algorithm ("lbfgs", "bfgs", "newton"), iter, tol_obj, history_size, jacobian.

title: Analyse Models — analyse
text: Calls cmdstanr::laplace(). embr-controlled: data, seed, init, mode, draws (niters), show_messages, show_exceptions. Common pass-throughs: jacobian, opt_args.

text: Named list mapping data column names type range specifications: 1L integer, 1 numeric, factor("") factor, TRUE logical. explicit check wanted, use range form c(0L, 100L) append NA allow missing values: c(0L, 100L, NA). Appending suffix column name requests rescaling transformation; original column replaced transformed version analysis dataset. * conventional continuous covariates + year date variables. Never apply suffix response. center scale character-vector arguments kept backwards compatibility emit deprecation warning; prefer select_data suffixes.

title: Define a Model — model
text: R expression (e.g. { ... }) character string R code evaluated post-fitting MCMC draws. access sampled parameters, modified data list, extras helpers log_lik_* res_*. expression typically defines named terms used downstream tooling: prediction[] predict.mb_analysis() fit[] fitted.mb_analysis() residuals log_lik[] information criteria prior sensitivity lprior[k] prior sensitivity scale fit[] model-specific: define match signature res_* log_lik_* helpers use (e.g. response-scale res_neg_binom, log-scale res_student log-normal data). Scalar derived quantities defined outside loop extractable via predict(analysis, new_data = character(0), term = "myScalar"). new_expr supplied string without wrapping braces added automatically. Setting new_expr_vec = TRUE vectorises -loop body via mcmcderive::expression_vectorize() substantial speed gains. Vectorisation silently falls back un-vectorised loop (error) loop body contains sum(), nested loops, dynamic range indexing eK[year[]:(year[] + n)]. Safe patterns: linear predictors built indexed terms, single-level (bSite[site[]]) multi-level (bParam[site[], annual[]]) indexing, link-function assignments (log(eY[]) <- ...), polynomial terms standardised predictors.

title: Define a Model — model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Stan model with a standardised continuous covariate and a site # random effect. count_model <- model(   code = count_code, # a string or read_file("stan/count.stan")   select_data = list(     count = 1L,     site = factor("a"),     `temperature*` = 1   ),   random_effects = list(z_bSite = "site"),   new_expr = {     bSite <- z_bSite * sSite     for (i in 1:nObs) {       log(eCount[i]) <- bIntercept + bTemp * temperature[i] +         bSite[site[i]]       prediction[i] <- eCount[i]       fit[i] <- eCount[i]       log_lik[i] <- log_lik_neg_binom(count[i], eCount[i], bPhi)     }   },   new_expr_vec = TRUE )  # Reshape multi-pass data into a matrix and add a per-row pass count # before the analysis runs; seed eAbundance with the observed totals. depletion_model <- model(   code = depletion_code,   select_data = list(     Pass1 = 1L, Pass2 = 1L, Pass3 = 1L,     site = factor("a")   ),   modify_data = function(data) {     Pass <- as.matrix(data[c("Pass1", "Pass2", "Pass3")])     data$Pass <- Pass     data$nPass <- ncol(Pass)     data$Pass1 <- data$Pass2 <- data$Pass3 <- NULL     data   },   gen_inits = function(data) {     list(eAbundance = rowSums(data$Pass, na.rm = TRUE) + 1)   } ) } # }
